sentences.sentence: Albert Einstein was born in 1879 in Germany.
sentences.reason: Albert Einstein was indeed born in Ulm, Germany, in 1879.

sentences.sentence: He won the Nobel Prize in 1921 for his Theory of Relativity.
sentences.reason: Einstein won the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics, but it was specifically awarded for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect, not explicitly for the Theory of Relativity.

sentences.sentence: Einstein developed the famous equation E=mc2.
sentences.reason: The mass-energy equivalence formula, E=mc², is one of Einstein's most famous contributions.

sentences.sentence: He moved to the United States in 1933 and worked at Princeton University.
sentences.reason: Einstein emigrated to the US in 1933 and took a position at the Institute for Advanced Study at Princeton.

sentences.sentence: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
sentences.reason: This is a persistent myth; the Great Wall is not visible from Earth orbit or the Moon with the unaided human eye.

sentences.sentence: Humans only use about 10 percent of their brain capacity.
sentences.reason: The 10% brain myth is scientifically inaccurate; brain imaging shows that humans use virtually all parts of the brain over a 24-hour period.

sentences.sentence: Einstein passed away in 1955 in New Jersey at age 77.
sentences.reason: Einstein died on April 18, 1955, in Princeton, New Jersey, at the age of 76 (he would have turned 77 later that year).
